title: Level 13: I2C Comm (TWI Master)
aim: To address multiple devices over the I2C (Two-Wire Interface - TWI) bus using only two synchronous pins: SCL and SDA.
objective: Understand the TWI Control mechanisms: TWCR, TWSR, and TWBR, generate a START/STOP condition, and address unique 7-bit slave devices.
theory:
The I2C Bus Architecture

Unlike UART (point-to-point) or SPI (multi-CS), I2C uses Open-Drain circuitry and unique addressing. Only SDA (Serial Data) and SCL (Serial Clock) are needed to connect up to 127 unique devices to the ATmega328P. Both lines must be pulled up to VCC with external resistors.

Master Control: TWCR

The TWI Control Register (TWCR) manages every single bus action. A transaction is initiated by generating a START condition: writing `(1<

Addressing Slaves

After a successful START, the master transmits the 7-bit Slave Address appended with a Read/Write bit (0 for Write, 1 for Read) out of TWDR. The designated slave responds by pulling SDA Low during the 9th clock cycle, known as the Acknowledge (ACK).

pretest.question: What happens if you never write a 1 to the TWINT bit in the TWCR register?
options: The transaction starts, but the flag isn't cleared
The I2C hardware remains stalled permanently, waiting to be cleared
An interrupt forces the bit to 0 automatically
pretest.explanation: Clearing the TWINT flag literally un-stalls the hardware state machine allowing the next action (like sending data) to legally execute.

pretest.question: What is the primary purpose of the DDRx register in the ATmega328P?
options: To configure pins as input or output
pretest.explanation: DDR configures the direction of each GPIO pin.

pretest.question: Which header file provides basic I/O register definitions for AVR?
pretest.explanation: <avr/io.h> provides register definitions for all AVR peripherals.

pretest.question: What does the volatile keyword tell the C compiler?
options: May change unexpectedly, so do not optimize reads
pretest.explanation: volatile prevents the compiler from caching the variable.

pretest.question: How many GPIO pins does the ATmega328P have in total?
pretest.explanation: 23 GPIO pins across Port B, C, and D.

pretest.question: What is the maximum clock frequency of the ATmega328P?
pretest.explanation: Supports up to 20MHz, though Arduino UNO uses 16MHz.

pretest.question: What is the purpose of sei()?
pretest.explanation: sei() sets the global interrupt enable flag in SREG.

pretest.question: Which AVR memory type retains data after power-off?
What is the width of each ATmega328P port register?
pretest.explanation: The ATmega328P is an 8-bit MCU; all port registers are 8 bits wide.

pretest.question: What is bit manipulation?
options: Operating on individual bits using bitwise operators
pretest.explanation: Bit manipulation uses AND, OR, XOR, and SHIFT.

procedure: Initialize the bus speed (SCL Frequency) using the TWI Bit Rate Register (TWBR).
Enable the TWI hardware explicitly (TWEN = 1) in TWCR.
Formally generate a START condition on the bus. (TWCR = (1<<TWINT)|(1<<TWSTA)|(1<<TWEN)).
Wait for the hardware to confirm it successfully grabbed the bus by checking when the TWINT bit sets to 1: `while (!(TWCR & (1<<TWINT)));`.
Load the Slave Address (e.g., 0x27) shifted left by 1 into TWDR. Set the LSB to 0 to WRITE.
Clear TWINT and enable TWI again to send the address: `TWCR = (1<<TWINT) | (1<<TWEN);`.
Wait again. After ACK, load real data into TWDR. Clear TWINT again.
After all data is sent, generate a STOP condition: `TWCR = (1<<TWINT)|(1<<TWEN)|(1<<TWSTO);`.

posttest.question: Why does I2C use 'Open Drain' configuration for its communication lines?
options: Devices never drive the lines logic HIGH; they only pull them LOW, avoiding short-circuits if two devices talk simultaneously
It operates better through internal voltage regulators
posttest.explanation: Any device on the bus can safely yank the line to Ground (0V). The pull-up resistors handle pulling it back to 5V.

posttest.question: Why is it good practice to use #define for pin numbers?
posttest.explanation: #define creates named constants for self-documenting code.

posttest.question: What tool converts C source to .hex for AVR?
posttest.explanation: avr-gcc compiles C to AVR machine code.

posttest.question: What is the difference between PORTB and PINB?
options: PORTB writes output; PINB reads actual pin voltages
posttest.explanation: PORTB drives outputs; PINB reads real-time pin states.

posttest.question: What happens to SRAM when power is removed?
What is the stack used for in AVR?
posttest.explanation: Stack stores return addresses and local variables.

posttest.question: Which is NOT a valid ATmega328P port?
posttest.explanation: ATmega328P has only Port B, C, and D. No Port A.

posttest.question: What is a watchdog timer used for?
posttest.explanation: WDT auto-resets MCU when not periodically fed.

posttest.question: What does the SREG register contain?
posttest.explanation: SREG holds CPU flags and the global interrupt enable bit.

posttest.question: In an ISR, interrupts are automatically:
posttest.explanation: AVR clears I-flag in SREG upon entering an ISR.

feedback: Flawless bus manipulation. Welcome to I2C—the standard backbone for Sensors, OLED displays, and EEPROM modules.
